title: System Drift

content.question: Which standard has quietly drifted that you haven't noticed?
sop: Write down your original standard or process for one key area (e.g. response time, content quality, follow-up).
Compare it honestly to what's actually happening today, rate the Loss of Structure as a percentage (see calculation, page 4)
Identify how many months since the last real audit (see calculation, page 4)
Reset the standard back to its original bar, or consciously decide to update it.
Put a recurring audit on the calendar, weekly for fast-moving areas, monthly for slower ones.
questions: Which standard has quietly drifted that you haven't noticed?
What would a stranger notice is wrong with your systems that you've stopped seeing?
How long has it been since you compared what you're actually doing to what your original standard said you'd do?
content.equation_example: Loss of Structure equals 20% (share of your original standard no longer being followed), Time equals 4 (months since it was last enforced). Drift equals 5% per month. Higher/rising means your standards are eroding faster than you're maintaining them. Lower/falling means your systems are holding steady.
content.closing_statement: Unmaintained standards don't hold, they quietly erode.

caption:
Principle Nº 129: System Drift

Systems degrade without maintenance.

Ask yourself: Which standard has quietly drifted that you haven't noticed?
